message.content:
You are doing a read-only code review of the repo at "C:\repos\dflow plm\designflow-data-syncing" (~81 source/doc files, ~8k lines). Do NOT edit any files.

Read all .md documentation files and read through the entire source code (all .js/.ts files), skipping node_modules, .git, dist, coverage, and lock files. Read every source file — this is meant to be exhaustive, not a sample.

Find and report:
1. Bugs — actual defects: wrong logic, unhandled errors/promise rejections, race conditions in sync logic, data-loss risks (partial writes, missing transactions, overwriting newer data with older), security issues, null/undefined crashes.
2. Inefficient code — N+1 queries, queries inside loops, syncing whole datasets when deltas would do, repeated work, missing batching, blocking synchronous calls.
3. Poorly written code — duplicated blocks, dead code, confusing names, functions doing too many things, copy-paste drift, inconsistent error handling, magic numbers.
4. Documentation problems — .md files that contradict the actual code or are clearly stale.

For each finding give: file path and line number, severity (high = real bug, data-loss risk, or security issue / medium = likely problem or significant inefficiency / low = style or minor cleanup), a one-sentence plain-English explanation of what's wrong and what it could cause, and confidence (certain / likely / possible). Only report things you actually verified by reading the code — no speculation.

Your final message is the deliverable: a structured list of findings grouped by severity, plus a 2-3 sentence overall assessment of the codebase's health.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:mcp-builder: Guide for creating high-quality MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers that enable LLMs to interact with external services through well-designed tools. Use when building MCP servers to integrate external APIs or services, whether in Python (FastMCP) or Node/TypeScript (MCP SDK).
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	# Designflow Data Syncing
2	
3	Service responsible for synchronization flows between Designflow system and Coldlion.  
4	This API is protected with an `X-API-Key` header and is intended for system-to-system usage rather than direct public access.
5	
6	## Stack
7	
8	- Node.js (CommonJS)
9	- Express
10	- PostgreSQL + Sequelize
11	- Axios-based integrations (Coldlion)
12	- Jest (unit) + Playwright (e2e scaffold)
13	
14	> Canonical guide: see [`AGENTS.md`](AGENTS.md) for architecture, data model, deployment, and
15	> known quirks. This README is the quick-start entry point.
16	
17	## Project Layout
18	
19	- `index.js`: server bootstrap and API key gate
20	- `routes/lib.controller.js`: sync-related routes
21	- `routes/health.router.js`: health endpoint
22	- `models/`: DB models and connection
23	- `controllers/`, `helpers/`: sync logic
24	
25	## Documentation
26	
27	| Doc | Contents |
28	|---|---|
29	| [`docs/api-reference.md`](docs/api-reference.md) | Data Sync API route inventory |
30	
31	## Security
32	
33	Requests must include:
34	
35	- Header: `x-api-key: <DFLOW_SYNC_API_KEY>`
36	
37	If the key is missing/invalid, the server rejects the request.
38	
39	## Environment Setup
40	
41	This repo loads `.env.<NODE_ENV>` via `config/env.js`.
42	
43	Available env files:
44	
45	- `.env.localhost`
46	- `.env.example`
47	
48	Important variables:
49	
50	- `PORT` (default `5001`)
51	- `DFLOW_SYNC_API_KEY`
52	- `SCHEMA`
53	- database variables required by `models/db`
54	
55	## Data encoding (UTF-8) — read before seeding or bulk-loading
56	
57	This system stores Chinese, accented Latin (`Décor`, `Cortázar`), `©`, and smart
58	quotes. **All of it must move as UTF-8.** The connection now pins
59	`client_encoding: 'UTF8'` in `models/db.js` so the app cannot introduce mojibake,
60	but that does **not** protect data loaded outside the app.
61	
62	**Never** pipe a SQL/CSV dump into Postgres through `cmd.exe` or any console on the
63	default Windows code page (**CP437**). Doing so silently scrambles every non-ASCII
64	value (`中国` → `Σ╕¡σ¢╜`, `Décor` → `D├⌐cor`). This already happened once and
65	corrupted thousands of rows in the `designflow_dev` schema.
66	
67	When re-seeding / bulk-loading, use a UTF-8-safe path:
68	
69	- `pg_dump` / `pg_restore` in custom or directory format, **or**
70	- `psql` with `\copy` and `client_encoding=UTF8` — e.g.
71	  `psql "host=… dbname=… options=-c client_encoding=UTF8"`, or run
72	  `SET client_encoding TO 'UTF8';` before `\copy`.
73	- If you must use a Windows console, run `chcp 65001` first and confirm the source
74	  file is UTF-8.
75	
76	After any seed, scan for the box-drawing/garbage bytes that signal mojibake to
77	confirm a clean load. Full incident write-up:
78	`designflow-backend/AGENTS.md` §14 ("designflow_dev CP437 mojibake").
79	
80	## Install
81	
82	```bash
83	npm install
84	```
85	
86	## Run
87	
88	```bash
89	npm run start
90	npm run start:sandbox
91	npm run start:production
92	```
93	
94	## Scripts
95	
96	- `npm run start`: runs **`npm test`** first, then dev server with `NODE_ENV=localhost`
97	- `npm run start:sandbox`: runs **`npm test`** first, then dev server with `NODE_ENV=sandbox`
98	- `npm run start:sandbox-albert`: dev server with `NODE_ENV=sandbox-albert` — **no test gate**
99	- `npm run start:production`: runs **`npm test`** first, then production runtime
100	- `npm test`: runs Jest unit tests (`jest.config.js`)
101	- `npm run test:e2e` / `test:e2e:ui` / `test:e2e:report`: Playwright (boilerplate scaffold)
102	
103	> Note: there is no `lint`/`format` script in `package.json`. Earlier versions of this README
104	> listed ESLint/Prettier/Husky and `npm run lint` / `npm run format`; those do not exist in the
105	> repo today.
106	
107	## Unit tests
108	
109	Unit tests use [Jest](https://jestjs.io/). Configuration lives in `jest.config.js` at the repo root and matches the other Designflow backend and BFF services. Jest is a **production dependency** so `start:sandbox` / `start:production` can run `npm test` after `npm ci --omit=dev`.
110	
111	```bash
112	npm test
113	```
114	
115	Useful variants:
116	
117	- Watch mode: `npx jest --watch`
118	- One file: `npx jest tests/unit/sanity.test.js`
119	
120	## Health Check
121	
122	`GET /api/health`
123	
124	> Include `x-api-key` even for health checks because middleware validates all incoming requests.
125	
126	## System interconnectivity
127	
128	This repo is the Coldlion/system synchronization boundary for the Designflow PLM
129	system. It feeds item, merch group, production order, vendor, customer, and
130	prepack-association data into the shared database used by the application
131	services. See [`docs/system-interconnectivity.md`](docs/system-interconnectivity.md)
132	for the sync-specific map and [`docs/system-architecture.md`](docs/system-architecture.md)
133	for the full multi-repo system map.
134

1	# AGENTS.md — designflow-data-syncing
2	
3	Canonical operating guide for developers and AI sessions working in this repo.
4	Read this first; it should give you a working mental model in under 5 minutes.
5	
6	## 1. Project summary
7	
8	`designflow-data-syncing` is a Node/Express + Sequelize + PostgreSQL **integration service** in
9	the Designflow PLM platform. Its job is to pull master/operational data from an external system
10	called **Coldlion** (over HTTP via Axios) and upsert it into the shared Designflow PostgreSQL
11	database — item headers/details, merch-group details, production orders, RFQ data, and external
12	vendor/customer records. It is a private, system-to-system API: every request must carry a valid
13	`X-API-Key` header; there is no user-facing UI. It writes to the **same PostgreSQL schema** used
14	by `designflow-backend` (see §6), so its Sequelize models are deliberately a near-copy of that
15	repo's.
16	
17	## Multi-model AI note
18	
19	There is no universal ignore-file standard across AI coding tools.
20	
21	`.claudeignore` works for Claude Code.
22	
23	When using any other AI tool, paste this file as your first message and follow the instructions in the "What to ignore" section.
24	
25	## Documentation map: what to read for each task
26	
27	Always start with:
28	
29	- `AGENTS.md`
30	
31	Then load additional docs only when relevant:
32	
33	| Task / question | Read these docs | Usually do not need |
34	|---|---|---|
35	| Quick repo orientation | `README.md`, `AGENTS.md` | everything else |
36	| Change a sync endpoint / sync logic | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/api-reference.md`, `routes/lib.controller.js`, `models/lib.model.js`, `helpers/utility.js` | deploy docs unless deploy changes |
37	| Look up a sync route / its auth | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/api-reference.md` | unrelated code |
38	| Change Coldlion field → DB column mapping | `AGENTS.md` (§6), `helpers/utility.js` (`remapItemHeader`) | unrelated model files |
39	| Add/change a DB table or column | `AGENTS.md` (§5, §6, §9 quirks), `models/db/<table>.js`, `models/db.js` migration block | already-applied data |
40	| Change env vars / config / secrets | `AGENTS.md` (§8), `cloudbuild.yaml`, `config/env.js`, `config/db.config.js` | unrelated code |
41	| Local setup, run, test | `AGENTS.md` (§3, §10), `README.md` | deploy docs |
42	| Deploy / Docker / Cloud Build / rollback | `AGENTS.md` (§7) | local-only run notes |
43	| Re-seed / bulk-load the database (UTF-8) | `AGENTS.md` (§11), `README.md` ("Data encoding"), `designflow-backend/AGENTS.md` §14 | unrelated code |
44	| Investigate a bug/incident | `AGENTS.md` (§11), `README.md` | unrelated code |
45	| Claude Code session | `CLAUDE.md`, then `AGENTS.md` | other docs unless needed |
46	| Docs-only cleanup | `AGENTS.md`, `README.md` | source except to verify |
47	
48	The doc set is `AGENTS.md` + `README.md` + `docs/api-reference.md` (route inventory). There is
49	currently **no `HANDOFF.md`** — if one appears, it is required reading for continuation work.
50	Update this table whenever a doc is added, removed, or repurposed.
51	
52	## 2. Repository structure
53	
54	| Path | What it is | Ownership |
55	|---|---|---|
56	| `index.js` | Express bootstrap: `x-user-authorization`→`authorization` swap, CORS, **`X-API-Key` gate**, mounts `/` → `routes/`, `app.listen` (default port **5001**) | code we own |
57	| `config/env.js` | dotenv loader; resolves `.env.${NODE_ENV}` | code we own |
58	| `config/db.config.js` | PostgreSQL connection params; throws if a required DB var is missing | code we own |
59	| `routes/index.js` | Root router; mounts `/` (`lib.controller.js`) and `/api` (`health.router.js`) | code we own |
60	| `routes/lib.controller.js` | Coldlion sync endpoints (item header/detail, merch group, prod orders, external vendor/customer, SSE stream) | code we own |
61	| `routes/health.router.js` | `GET /api/health` | code we own |
62	| `controllers/main.controller.js` | Thin HTTP handlers; delegate to `Customer` in `models/lib.model.js` | code we own |
63	| `models/lib.model.js` | Sync domain logic (~3k lines): Coldlion fetch + pagination + find-or-create/upsert | code we own |
64	| `models/db.js` | Sequelize bootstrap: connection pool, model registration, idempotent migration block. **Required at boot** (see §11) | code we own |
65	| `models/db/*.js` | Sequelize table definitions (one file per table), auto-loaded by `init-models.js` | code we own |
66	| `models/obj/*.module.js` | Light object/module wrappers for a few entities | code we own |
67	| `helpers/utility.js` | `remapItemHeader()` — Coldlion field names → internal DB columns | code we own |
68	| `helpers/multer.js` | Multer upload middleware | code we own |
69	| `helpers/imageMagick.service.js` | ImageMagick CLI wrapper for image processing | code we own |
70	| `tests/unit/*.test.js` | Jest unit tests (currently only a sanity test — see §11) | code we own |
71	| `e2e/*.spec.js` | Playwright boilerplate (not wired to this service — see §11) | code we own |
72	| `Dockerfile`, `cloudbuild.yaml` | Build + Cloud Run deploy | deployment files |
73	| `uploads/` | Runtime upload scratch dir | generated (ignored) |
74	| `node_modules/` | Installed deps (gitignored) | third-party |
75	
76	There is no separate ORM migration tool: **the Sequelize models are the schema source of
77	truth** (`sequelize.sync()` creates tables), with an idempotent `ALTER TABLE … ADD COLUMN IF
78	NOT EXISTS` block in `models/db.js` for additive column changes — same pattern as
79	`designflow-backend` and `designflow-tracking`.
80	
81	## 3. Prime Directive: custom-code boundary
82	
83	Our custom code lives here:
84	
85	- `index.js`, `config/`, `routes/`, `controllers/`, `models/`, `helpers/`
86	- `tests/`
87	- `Dockerfile`, `cloudbuild.yaml`
88	
89	Everything under `node_modules/` is third-party and must not be edited. The whole repo is
90	project-owned — there is no committed vendor/framework layer. New work follows the existing
91	**router → controller → model** layering; do not put DB logic in controllers or HTTP logic in
92	models.
93	
94	## 3a. Branch, sync, test, and PR workflow
95	
96	All feature work for this sandbox happens on `sandbox-albert`.
97	
98	- Work only on `sandbox-albert`; use `develop` only as the sync source and PR base.
99	- Never commit to, merge into, or open PRs against `main`. Do not force-push.
100	- Add/update a unit test for any function or behavior you create or change; run `npm test`
101	  before committing.
102	- Commit on `sandbox-albert`, push to `origin/sandbox-albert`, then keep (or create) the PR
103	  `sandbox-albert` → `develop`.
104	
105	## 4. Core modification inventory
106	
107	No files outside the project-owned areas above were modified. The whole repo is project-owned;
108	there is no vendored or generated code checked in.
109	
110	| File | Change made | Why it was necessary | Risk during upgrades |
111	|---|---|---|---|
112	| — | — | — | — |
113	
114	## 5. Task-to-file navigation: what to edit for common changes
115	
116	| Task | Files to touch | Files NOT to touch |
117	|---|---|---|
118	| Add/modify a sync endpoint | `routes/lib.controller.js`, `controllers/main.controller.js`, `models/lib.model.js` | `node_modules/` |
119	| Change Coldlion→DB field mapping | `helpers/utility.js` (`remapItemHeader`) | already-applied data |
120	| Add a DB table | new `models/db/<table>.js` (+ association in `models/db/init-models.js`) | applied rows |
121	| Add a column to a table | the `models/db/<table>.js` field **and** an `ALTER TABLE … ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS` line in `models/db.js` (quote mixed-case identifiers — see §11) | rename existing columns |
122	| Change the API-key gate / CORS | `index.js` | route files |
123	| Add a new env-driven config | `config/env.js`, `config/db.config.js`, `cloudbuild.yaml` (`--set-secrets`/`--set-env-vars`) | production `.env` directly |
124	
125	## 6. Data model and external identifiers
126	
127	This service reads from Coldlion and writes to the **shared** Designflow PostgreSQL schema
128	(`process.env.SCHEMA`, e.g. `designflow_dev` / `designflow`). The `models/db/` directory is a
129	near-duplicate of `designflow-backend/models/db/` (no shared npm package — see Notes).
130	
131	| Entity / system | Identifier | Where defined | Notes |
132	|---|---|---|---|
133	| Item header | `item_*` columns | `models/db/itemHeader.js` | Primary sync target; populated from Coldlion item feed |
134	| Item detail / size / depth / attachment | per-table keys | `models/db/itemDetail.js`, `itemSize.js`, `itemDepth.js`, `itemAttachment.js` | Item sub-records |
135	| Merch group | merch-group keys | `models/db/merchGroup.js` | Merchandising hierarchy |
136	| Production order | header/detail keys | `models/db/ProdOrderHeader.js`, `ProdOrderDetail.js` | Manufacturing orders |
137	| RFQ\* | per-table keys | `models/db/RFQ*.js` | RFQ workflow tables (shared shape with backend) |
138	| External customer / vendor / api | external keys | `models/db/externalCustomer.js`, `externalVendor.js`, `externalApi.js` | `externalApi` row holds Coldlion host + path |
139	| Coldlion (external system) | `X-API-Key: COLDLION_API_KEY` | called from `models/lib.model.js` via Axios | Source ERP/commerce system; paged (`?page=N&sort=createdtime,desc`) |
140	| Company code mapping | `EDGEHOME → 1`, else `2` | `helpers/utility.js` | Hardcoded mapping during remap |
141	| DB schema | `SCHEMA` (env) | `index.js`, models | e.g. `designflow_dev`, `designflow` |
142	| This service's API key | `DFLOW_SYNC_API_KEY` | `index.js` gate | Required on every request |
143	
144	Notes: `models/db/` is **physically duplicated** between this repo and `designflow-backend`
145	(and overlaps with `designflow-tracking`). There is no `@designflow/models` shared package, so a
146	schema change must be made in each repo that owns a copy. Verify by diffing the `models/db/`
147	folders across repos.
148	
149	## 7. Container and service inventory
150	
151	| Service | Purpose | Managed by | Image/source |
152	|---|---|---|---|
153	| data-syncing (this repo) | Private Cloud Run service (`--no-allow-unauthenticated`); Coldlion → Postgres sync | Cloud Build → Cloud Run | `${_REGION}-docker.pkg.dev/${PROJECT_ID}/${_ARTIFACT_REPO_NAME}/${_APP_ENV}/${_APP_NAME}:${BUILD_ID}-${COMMIT_SHA}` (Dockerfile, `node:lts-alpine`) |
154	| PostgreSQL | Shared Designflow database (via `DB_HOST`/`DB_PORT`) | platform (Cloud SQL) | — |
155	| Coldlion | External source system polled over HTTPS | external (third party) | REST API; `COLDLION_API_KEY` |
156	
157	Service name, region, VPC connector, memory, CPU, port, and schema are supplied by the Cloud
158	Build **trigger substitutions** (`_SERVICE_NAME`, `_REGION`, `_PORT`, `_DB_SCHEMA`, …), not
159	hard-coded in the repo. Deploy is private with a VPC connector and a service account.
160	
161	## 8. Credentials and environment
162	
163	`config/env.js` loads `.env.<NODE_ENV>`. `config/db.config.js` throws if any required DB var is
164	missing. **No secret values appear in this repo** — local secrets live in gitignored
165	`.env.<env>` files; production secrets live in Google Secret Manager and are injected by
166	`cloudbuild.yaml`.
167	
168	| Variable | Purpose | Stored where (prod) | Dev | Prod |
169	|---|---|---|---|---|
170	| `DB_HOST`, `DB_PORT`, `DB_USER`, `DB_PASSWORD`, `DB_NAME` | PostgreSQL connection | Secret Manager (`--set-secrets`) | yes | yes |
171	| `DFLOW_SYNC_API_KEY` | Gates every inbound request (`X-API-Key`) | Secret Manager (`--set-secrets`) | yes | yes |
172	| `COLDLION_API_KEY` | Auth for outbound Coldlion API calls | Secret Manager (`--set-secrets`) | yes | yes |
173	| `SCHEMA` | PostgreSQL schema name | `--set-env-vars` (`_DB_SCHEMA` substitution) | yes | yes |
174	| `NODE_ENV` | Selects `.env.<NODE_ENV>` | `package.json` start script / build arg | yes | yes |
175	| `PORT` | Express listen port (default 5001) | Cloud Run `--port` (`_PORT`) | optional | yes |
176	| `WEB_URL`, `API_ADDRESS` | Reference/startup-log values | `.env.<env>` (not set in `cloudbuild.yaml`) | optional | optional |
177	
178	Never put secrets in `--set-env-vars` (that flag is a full replacement on every deploy). Secrets
179	go in `--set-secrets` (Secret Manager), which persist independently.
180	
181	## 9. What to ignore
182	
183	`node_modules/`, `dist/`, `coverage/`, `test-results/`, `playwright-report/`, `blob-report/`,
184	`playwright/.cache/`, `uploads/`, `*.log`, `.idea/`, `.vscode/`, and `.env*` files. See
185	`.claudeignore` (mirrored by `.cursorignore`). Large media and build artifacts are also in
186	`.gitignore`.
187	
188	## 10. Build, run, and test
189	
190	Runtime: Node.js LTS (Alpine in Docker). Default port **5001**.
191	
192	| Command | What it does |
193	|---|---|
194	| `npm install` | Install dependencies |
195	| `npm test` | Jest unit tests (`tests/**/*.test.js`) |
196	| `npm run start` | `npm test` then dev server, `NODE_ENV=localhost` (nodemon) |
197	| `npm run start:sandbox` | `npm test` then dev server, `NODE_ENV=sandbox` (nodemon) |
198	| `npm run start:sandbox-albert` | dev server, `NODE_ENV=sandbox-albert` (nodemon) — **no test gate** |
199	| `npm run start:production` | `npm test` then `node index.js`, `NODE_ENV=production` |
200	| `npm run test:e2e` / `:ui` / `:report` | Playwright (boilerplate only — see §11) |
201	
202	There is **no** `lint`, `lint:fix`, or `format` script in `package.json` (an older README listed
203	them; corrected). There is no separate build step beyond `npm install`.
204	
205	## 11. Intentional quirks and non-obvious decisions
206	
207	### Models are duplicated, not shared, with `designflow-backend`
208	
209	Looks like: `models/db/` should be a shared package imported by both services.
210	
211	Actually: each repo keeps its own physical copy of the Sequelize model files.
212	
213	Why: the services deploy independently and there is no internal package registry; copying was
214	the pragmatic choice when the sync service was split out.
215	
216	Do not change because: "fixing" this into a shared package is a cross-repo migration, not a solo
217	edit. Until then, a schema change must be applied in every repo that owns a copy.
218	
219	### `ADD COLUMN` DDL must quote mixed-case identifiers
220	
221	Looks like: you can write `ALTER TABLE "itemHeader" ADD COLUMN itemNewCol TEXT`.
222	
223	Actually: unquoted mixed-case identifiers fold to lowercase in Postgres DDL, creating an orphan
224	column Sequelize (which quotes everything) can never find.
225	
226	Why: this exact mistake caused an outage in the sibling backend (`designflow-backend` AGENTS.md
227	§14, SYS-002).
228	
229	Do not change because: an unquoted `ADD COLUMN` silently creates the wrong column.
230	
231	### UTF-8 everywhere; never load data through a Windows console
232	
233	Looks like: any `psql`/CSV import path is fine for re-seeding.
234	
235	Actually: the connection pins `client_encoding: 'UTF8'`, but data loaded **outside** the app
236	through `cmd.exe` (CP437) silently mojibakes every non-ASCII value.
237	
238	Why: this already corrupted thousands of rows in `designflow_dev`. Full write-up:
239	`designflow-backend/AGENTS.md` §14, and `README.md` "Data encoding".
240	
241	Do not change because: a non-UTF-8 load path re-corrupts Chinese/accented data. Use
242	`pg_dump`/`pg_restore` (custom format) or `psql` with `client_encoding=UTF8`.
243	
244	### `start:sandbox-albert` skips the test gate
245	
246	Looks like: an oversight versus the other `start:*` scripts.
247	
248	Actually: it intentionally omits the `npm test &&` prefix so the sandbox can boot without the
249	suite.
250	
251	Why: fast local iteration on the sandbox branch.
252	
253	Do not change because: it is deliberate — but run `npm test` yourself before committing, since
254	this script will not.
255	
256	### Dockerfile `EXPOSE 5000` vs app default `5001`
257	
258	Looks like: a port bug.
259	
260	Actually: `EXPOSE` is documentation only; Cloud Run routes to the container port given by
261	`--port=${_PORT}` (trigger substitution), and `app.listen` honors `PORT`. The literal `5000` in
262	the Dockerfile is not what production uses.
263	
264	Verify: check the `_PORT` value on the Cloud Build trigger and the `PORT` in each `.env.<env>`.
265	This is an UNKNOWN at the repo level (set on the trigger).
266	
267	## 12. Critical incidents
268	
269	### designflow_dev CP437 mojibake (cross-service)
270	
271	What happened: a SQL/CSV dump was piped into Postgres through a Windows console on the CP437
272	code page, scrambling every non-ASCII value (`中国` → `Σ╕¡σ¢╜`) across ~20+ text columns in the
273	`designflow_dev` schema.
274	
275	Impact: thousands of corrupted rows in the shared schema this service writes to.
276	
277	Root cause: non-UTF-8 console encoding on a bulk load path outside the application.
278	
279	Recovery: in-place repair of affected rows; `client_encoding: 'UTF8'` pinned on the connection.
280	
281	Rule added to prevent recurrence: never bulk-load through `cmd.exe`; use UTF-8-safe paths only
282	(see §11 and `README.md`). Full write-up lives in `designflow-backend/AGENTS.md` §14.
283

1	# Designflow Data Syncing API Reference
2	
3	This service handles synchronization flows between Designflow and Coldlion.
4	It is intended for system-to-system use, not direct browser or public use.
5	
6	In deployed environments callers normally use the BFF:
7	
8	```text
9	https://api.designflow.app/api/data_sync/<data-sync-path>
10	```
11	
12	Direct local base:
13	
14	```text
15	http://localhost:5001
16	```
17	
18	## Auth
19	
20	Every request must include:
21	
22	```text
23	X-API-Key: <DFLOW_SYNC_API_KEY>
24	```
25	
26	The middleware validates this key before routing, including health checks.
27	
28	## Route Mounts
29	
30	| Mount path | Router | Purpose |
31	|---|---|---|
32	| `/` | `lib.controller.js` | Coldlion sync endpoints |
33	| `/api` | `health.router.js` | Health check |
34	
35	## Health
36	
37	| Method | Path | Auth | Purpose |
38	|---|---|---|---|
39	| `GET` | `/api/health` | `X-API-Key` | Service health |
40	
41	## Sync Routes
42	
43	Mounted at the service root.
44	
45	| Method | Path | Auth | Purpose |
46	|---|---|---|---|
47	| `GET` | `/getItemHeaderFromCL` | `X-API-Key` | Pull item headers from Coldlion |
48	| `GET` | `/getItemDetailFromCL` | `X-API-Key` | Pull item details from Coldlion |
49	| `POST` | `/getItem` | `X-API-Key` | Get item information |
50	| `GET` | `/getMerchgroupDetailFromCL` | `X-API-Key` | Pull merch group detail from Coldlion |
51	| `POST` | `/streamMerchgroupDetailFromCL` | `X-API-Key` | Stream merch group detail sync |
52	| `GET` | `/getMerchGroupHeaders` | `X-API-Key` | Get merch group headers |
53	| `GET` | `/getProdOrderHeader` | `X-API-Key` | Pull production order headers |
54	| `GET` | `/getExternalVendor` | `X-API-Key` | Pull external vendors |
55	| `GET` | `/getExternalCustomer` | `X-API-Key` | Pull external customers |
56	| `GET` | `/getProdOrderDetail` | `X-API-Key` | Pull production order details |
57	| `GET` | `/syncPrepackAssociations` | `X-API-Key` | Sync prepack associations |
58	
59	## Contract Status
60	
61	This is a private integration API. Keep it service-token/API-key protected. Do
62	not expose these routes as a general external data API without adding stable DTOs,
63	rate limits, and audit logging.
64	
65	## Cross-repo system map
66	
67	This service feeds data into the shared PLM schema that is later consumed by
68	core backend, item-master, and tracking features. See
69	[`system-interconnectivity.md`](system-interconnectivity.md) for the
70	data-sync-specific interconnectivity map and
71	[`system-architecture.md`](system-architecture.md) for the full
72	multi-repo architecture.
73

1	# System Interconnectivity
2	
3	The data-syncing service is the Coldlion/system synchronization boundary. It is
4	not a normal user-facing application API. It is private in deployed environments,
5	protected by `X-API-Key`, and reachable through the BFF as `/api/data_sync/*`
6	when needed.
7	
8	## Runtime Position
9	
10	```mermaid
11	flowchart LR
12	  BFF["designflow-bff"]
13	  Sync["designflow-data-syncing"]
14	  CL["Coldlion"]
15	  DB["PostgreSQL shared PLM schema"]
16	  Core["designflow-backend"]
17	  ItemMaster["designflow-item-master"]
18	  Tracking["designflow-tracking"]
19	
20	  BFF -->|/api/data_sync/*| Sync
21	  Sync <--> CL
22	  Sync --> DB
23	  Core -.reads synced data.-> DB
24	  ItemMaster -.reads synced data.-> DB
25	  Tracking -.reads synced data.-> DB
26	```
27	
28	## Sync Ownership
29	
30	| Sync area | Routes |
31	|---|---|
32	| Item headers/details | `/getItemHeaderFromCL`, `/getItemDetailFromCL`, `/getItem` |
33	| Merch groups | `/getMerchgroupDetailFromCL`, `/streamMerchgroupDetailFromCL`, `/getMerchGroupHeaders` |
34	| Production orders | `/getProdOrderHeader`, `/getProdOrderDetail` |
35	| External references | `/getExternalVendor`, `/getExternalCustomer` |
36	| Prepack associations | `/syncPrepackAssociations` |
37	
38	## Inter-Service Couplings
39	
40	Data-sync writes or refreshes data used by the other services:
41	
42	- Item Library and Item Detail in `designflow-item-master`
43	- RFQ/admin/factory/customer workflows in `designflow-backend`
44	- Production tracking and prepack associations in `designflow-tracking`
45	
46	Because the coupling is through the shared database, changes to sync shape,
47	normalization, or identifiers can surface later as application issues in another
48	service even when no direct HTTP call exists between them.
49

content.content:
# Designflow PLM System Architecture This document maps the Designflow product lifecycle management system across the local repositories under `D:\repos\dflow`. It is written from the code as it exists in this workspace, not from the GitHub repo names alone. The important correction to the informal "five repo" model is that the runtime PLM system has six active application/service repos locally: - `designflow-frontend` - `designflow-bff` - `designflow-backend` - `designflow-item-master` - `designflow-tracking` - `designflow-data-syncing` The original five named repos are the main subject, but `designflow-item-master` is an active service in the request path. The frontend calls it through the BFF, and the BFF has a first-class `item_master` service mapping. Any complete system map should include it. ## Executive Summary Designflow is a Cloud Run based PLM application. The browser runs a static Angular SPA. The SPA does not call private backend services directly in deployed environments; it calls the BFF at `api.*.designflow.app`. The BFF proxies each request to one of the private domain services, adding a Cloud Run OIDC token for service-to-service invocation while preserving the user's Designflow JWT in `X-User-Authorization`. Most services use: - Node.js CommonJS - Express - PostgreSQL - Sequelize - `SCHEMA` to select the logical database schema - RS256 Designflow JWTs for user identity and role checks The main persistent coupling between services is the shared PostgreSQL schema. The HTTP/API coupling is intentionally centralized through the BFF. ## High-Level Runtime View ```mermaid flowchart LR Browser["Browser

content.text:
# Code Review: designflow-data-syncing

## HIGH severity

**H1. Item-header, attachment, and merch-group-header syncs silently write nothing**
`models/lib.model.js:61-114` — `findOrCreate()` only contains an `if (schemaName === "merchGroup")` branch, yet it is called with `'itemHeader'` (line 214), `'itemAttachment'` (line 229), and `'merchGroupHeaders'` (line 637). For those, the loop does nothing and returns `[]`, so the `/getItemHeaderFromCL` and image-sync flows fetch everything from Coldlion and then discard it without a single DB write or any error. **Confidence: certain.**

**H2. Image upload calls a route that doesn't exist — on its own server**
`models/lib.model.js:497-517` — `uploadToGCS()` POSTs to `http://localhost:5001/uploadToGCS`, which is this service's own port, and no such route exists in `routes/`. The 404 is swallowed (returns `undefined`), then line 319 calls `publicUrl.replace(...)` on `undefined` → TypeError, aborting the whole image sync. **Confidence: certain** (within this repo; only works if some other process squats on port 5001).

**H3. Vendor/customer "update" step crashes on an undefined variable**
`models/lib.model.js:836-840` and `867-871` — inside the `.then` of `getExternalVendor`/`getExternalCustomer`, the where-condition references `data[i]`, but `i` is not defined in that scope → ReferenceError on every run. Result: existing vendor/customer rows are **never updated**, only brand-new rows inserted, and the error is returned as a 200 "success" body. **Confidence: certain.**

**H4. `findOrCreateWithSSE` creates duplicate merchGroup rows when DB data is newer**
`models/lib.model.js:161-170` — `modTime: { [Op.lte]: modTime }` is added to the `findOrCreate` *where* clause. If the existing DB row has a newer `modTime` than the incoming record, the lookup misses and a **duplicate row with older data** is created (the `merchGroup` table has no unique constraint on the composite keys — `models/db/merchGroup.js`). **Confidence: certain logic; likely in practice.**

**H5. Coldlion freeform/date fields are mapped and then silently nulled**
`helpers/utility.js:50-55 vs 127-133` — the object literal sets `udf_freeform_01..04` (and `udf_freeform_03` = brandAssuranceNo at line 103) from Coldlion data, then *later duplicate keys* in the same literal overwrite them with `null` (last key wins in JS). Every synced item permanently loses those values. **Confidence: certain.**

**H6. Division-code mapping is always 2**
`helpers/utility.js:25-32` — `if (item.divisionCode == 'divisionCode')` compares against the literal string `'divisionCode'`, which is never true, so every item gets `div_code_fk = 2`. (The mutation-inside-mapping `item.companyCode = (function(){...})()` style at lines 17-24 is also a side-effecting hazard.) **Confidence: certain.**

**H7. Batch splitter drops up to ~749 items from image sync**
`models/lib.model.js:223-230` — `processSplitter = Math.round(allItems.length / 1500)` uses `round` instead of `ceil`; e.g., 2,200 items → 1 batch of 1,500 and 700 items never processed. (`startingNum`/`endingNum` are also never updated, so the progress log always prints 0/0.) **Confidence: certain.**

**H8. Retry loop can spin forever and crash on undefined**
`models/lib.model.js:446-481` — `while (j < 5 || parseInt(head.headers['content-length']) == 200)` confuses HTTP status with content-length: any file whose size is exactly 200 bytes loops forever. `uploadVerification` (line 483) swallows network errors and returns `undefined`, so `head.headers` / `GCSFile.status` (line 415) throw TypeErrors, and `retryUpload` can fall off the end returning `undefined` while callers read `result.skipped` (lines 420, 431). **Confidence: certain.**

**H9. File write race: upload starts before the image is on disk**
`models/lib.model.js:519-529` — `storePictures` uses callback-style `fs.writeFile` and returns immediately (the `await` is meaningless), then `uploadToGCS` is invoked with the file path before the write completes — truncated/missing uploads. **Confidence: certain.**

**H10. Hardcoded schemas pin two tables to the wrong database schema in every environment**
`models/db/GridLayout.js:95` (`schema: 'designflow_sandbox'`) and `models/db/viewlayout.js:44` (`schema: 'rfq_backoffice13_prod'`) override the env `SCHEMA`; with `sequelize.sync()` at boot these tables are created/read in sandbox/prod schemas regardless of environment — cross-environment data bleed. **Confidence: certain** (hardcoding), likely (impact).

## MEDIUM severity

**M1. `process.env.schema` (lowercase) vs `SCHEMA` (uppercase)** — ~18 model files (`models/db/users.js:82`, `itemAttachment.js:62`, `companyCode.js:22`, etc.) use `process.env.schema` while `models/db.js:11` and deployment use `SCHEMA`. On case-sensitive Linux/Cloud Run, `schema` is undefined and those models silently fall back to the default search path — potential writes to the wrong schema. `lib.model.js:984` checking *both* spellings confirms the inconsistency is real. **Confidence: likely.**

**M2. `productions.js` is a copy-paste of `RFQItemStatus.js`** — `models/db/productions.js:3` defines model `'RFQItemStatus'` against table `RFQItemStatus`; `sequelize.define` runs twice for the same model name and there is no productions model at all. **Confidence: certain.**

**M3. Unhandled async bootstrap in `models/db.js`** — `sequelize.authenticate()` (line 30) is not awaited so the try/catch can never fire; `sequelize.sync()` (line 39) is fire-and-forget; `module.exports = db` only works because the async function happens to contain no awaits (adding one breaks every consumer); `throw 'msg', error` (line 34) is a comma-expression. **Confidence: certain.**

**M4. `init-models.js` race + unhandled rejection** — `models/db/init-models.js:32-68`: the `UDFTable.findAll()` that wires all model associations is fire-and-forget with **no `.catch`** (DB hiccup at boot → unhandled promise rejection) and completes *after* the models are returned, so early requests using includes (e.g., `getItemInfo`'s `itemAttachments`) can fail right after startup. It also dispatches association method names straight from DB rows (`models[x][row.UDFTable_associate_type](...)`) — a bad row throws. **Confidence: certain (code), likely (impact).**

**M5. `getProdOrderDetail` is an N+1 firehose and double-sends the response** — `models/lib.model.js:897-946`: loads **every** ProdOrderHeader ever synced, makes one Coldlion HTTP call per order, bulk-inserts, then re-updates **every row individually** in a loop; on error it calls `result(null, err)` *and* the outer `result(null, 'Data was updated')` still runs → callback fired twice → `ERR_HTTP_HEADERS_SENT`. Uses `data.length && data` (wrong order; throws if `data` is undefined). **Confidence: certain.**

**M6. Errors are returned as success** — throughout `lib.model.js` (lines 239, 547, 644, 794, 845, 944 …) the pattern is `result(null, err)`, so controllers send the raw error object with HTTP 200; callers/schedulers cannot detect failed syncs, and internal error details leak to the client. **Confidence: certain.**

**M7. Per-record N+1 upserts in merchGroup sync** — `findOrCreate` (lines 73-100) and `findOrCreateWithSSE` (line 167) issue a `findOne`/`findOrCreate` + `update` per record instead of batching/`bulkCreate updateOnDuplicate`; `getAllMerchGroupDetails` multiplies this across 3 divisions × 10 type codes. **Confidence: certain.**

**M8. Delta filters that miss modifications** — `getProdOrderHeader` (line 778) pulls only orders `createdFrom` last 3 days with `ignoreDuplicates` (header *modifications* are never re-synced); `getExternalVendor`/`getExternalCustomer` (lines 822, 855) use `createdFrom=today` so anything created yesterday while the job didn't run is lost forever; `getItemDetailFromCL` (line 950) pulls the **entire** itemDetails dataset every run yet only inserts (`ignoreDuplicates`) — existing detail rows are never updated. **Confidence: certain.**

**M9. Whole-dataset memory pressure in image sync** — `getItemIMGData` (lines 283-444) accumulates full base64 image payloads in `images[]`/`thumbnail[]` arrays and dedupes with `Array.includes` on multi-MB strings (O(n²) over blobs), while `getAllitems` (line 607) loads every itemHeader row with attachments into memory. **Confidence: certain.**

**M10. `getAllDataFromAPI`/`getData` swallow errors and return `undefined`** — lines 53-60, 803-818: the axios rejection handler just logs (`console.log(console.log(error))` — which itself logs `undefined`) and returns nothing, so every caller then crashes on `.content`/`.length` of undefined, far from the actual cause. **Confidence: certain.**

**M11. `config/env.js:7` crashes if `NODE_ENV` is unset** — `(process.env.NODE_ENV).toLowerCase()` → TypeError before any helpful message. **Confidence: certain.**

**M12. Dead-but-broken endpoints/functions** — `Customer.getMG`/`getIMG` (lib.model.js:530-569) call `findOrCreateMG`, **which is defined nowhere** → guaranteed ReferenceError; they're identical copies of each other, unrouted (controller exports `findAllMG`/`findAllIMG`/`findMerchgroupDetailFromCLOld` have no routes), and `getMG` (242-282) is itself a byte-for-byte copy of `getItemHeaderData` that even reuses `remapItemHeader` for merch-group data. `remapProdHeader` (`helpers/utility.js:246-261`) maps `prodHeaders` but reads from undefined `mgDetail` — would throw if ever called. **Confidence: certain.**

**M13. `helpers/imageMagick.service.js` requires the `imagemagick` package, which is not in `package.json`** — the file would crash on `require`; it's currently dead (never imported), and its cleanup logic deletes *every* file in `images/` whenever more than 2 exist, racing any concurrent sync. `helpers/multer.js` is likewise imported by `routes/lib.controller.js:4` but never used. **Confidence: certain.**

## LOW severity

- `index.js:32` — invalid API key returns **500** instead of 401/403, and the comparison is not timing-safe; CORS is `*`. (certain)
- `lib.model.js:983-988` — `associationSkipLiteral` builds raw SQL by string interpolation of env `SCHEMA`; inputs are internal today, but it's an injection-shaped pattern. (certain)
- `controllers/main.controller.js:73` — SSE error path writes `error: ...` which is not a valid SSE field; clients will never see it. Every handler also repeats the same copy-pasted "retrieving customers" error message, even for prod-order/vendor routes. (certain)
- `lib.model.js:216` — empty `else if (req.query.id == 2) {}` dead branch; `id` magic numbers 1/2/5 select sync behavior with no named constants. (certain)
- `lib.model.js:154` — `identifier = ...` implicit global, never used; `const { response } = require("express")` (line 8) unused. (certain)
- `lib.model.js:416, 474` — content-length checked with 6 redundant comparisons (`<= 0 || == 0 || == -1 || == null || == undefined || == ''`) on the same `parseInt` value. (certain)
- `models/obj/itemheader.module.js` is a 0-byte file and `itemAttachment.module.js` assigns an implicit global `item`; neither is imported anywhere — dead. (certain)
- `models/db.js:16` — pool `acquire: 600000` (10 min) and `connectTimeout: 300000` magic numbers. (certain)
- `config/env.js:5` comment claims supported NODE_ENVs are "local, develop, production" while real values are localhost/sandbox/sandbox-albert/production. (certain)

## Documentation problems

- **AGENTS.md §2 & §5 vs code (medium):** claims `models/db.js` contains an "idempotent migration block (`ALTER TABLE … ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS`)" and tells contributors to add columns there — `models/db.js` has no such block. (certain)
- **AGENTS.md §13 (low):** says `models/db.js` "is deleted in the working tree" — the file exists; stale incident note. (certain)
- **AGENTS.md §1 (low):** says the service syncs "RFQ data" — RFQ tables exist as model copies but no RFQ sync route or logic exists. Also says `lib.model.js` is "~3k lines" (it's 1,153). (certain)
- **AGENTS.md §2 (low):** describes `helpers/imageMagick.service.js` and `helpers/multer.js` as functional components without noting both are dead (and one has a missing dependency). (certain)

## Overall assessment

The scaffolding (routing, docs, env/secret handling, model duplication strategy) is reasonably organized, but the core sync logic in `models/lib.model.js` and `helpers/utility.js` is in poor health: three of the main sync flows are functionally broken end-to-end (item-header writes are a no-op, image upload targets a nonexistent route, vendor/customer updates crash on an undefined variable), and the field-mapping layer silently nulls or mis-maps data on every run. Error handling almost universally converts failures into HTTP 200 "successes," so these defects are likely going unnoticed in operation; the merchGroup path (the one flow with real upsert logic) is the healthiest, but even it can duplicate rows when local data is newer. I'd prioritize H1–H6 and adding real unit tests (the suite is currently a single `1+1` sanity test) before any new sync features.
